summary: Created a minimal M1 deterministic simulation kernel task DAG with SIM-001 READY and SIM-002 through SIM-006 DRAFT. The DAG focuses on IDs, Definition/State separation, WorldState v0, scheduler/order, command/event/query, deterministic RNG, save envelope, graph fixture/content compiler, cross Node/Worker determinism, invariants/golden tests, and explicit performance baselines.

tests_run.result: On branch chore/m1-task-graph before edits; no existing uncommitted changes were reported.
Only SIM-001 is dependency-ready: SIM-001 READY simulation_engineer.

tests_run.command: node -e strict schema-compatible field/type check for SIM-001..SIM-006 and project/goal-mode-state.json

tests_run.result: FOUNDATION and PROCESS tasks remain CLOSED; SIM-001 is READY; SIM-002 through SIM-006 are DRAFT.

decisions: Kept the docs/18 recommended six-task SIM shape with minimal dependency adjustments.
Set SIM-001 to READY because FOUNDATION-001 through FOUNDATION-008 are CLOSED and M0 Gate is cleared.
Kept later SIM tasks DRAFT so the lead can promote them only after dependencies and WIP gates are satisfied.
Made SIM-005 the M1 integration task for save round-trip, cross-environment hash, golden fixtures, and explicit performance baseline.
Kept all product/gameplay content outside M1 scope; SIM-006 is explicitly synthetic graph fixture data only.
risks: Task schema supports only one reviewer_role, while save/RNG work may need architecture review; SIM-004 and SIM-005 notes require routing to systems_architect if ADR or compatibility semantics change.
Several future package paths do not exist yet; they are allowed only so implementation tasks can create explicit packages such as save-format and content-runtime without expanding product scope.
No implementation tests such as pnpm check were run because this task changed only task/status/handoff JSON files.
requested_action: Review the M1 task DAG. If acceptable, route to lead_orchestrator to integrate and start SIM-001; otherwise return REQUEST_CHANGES with exact task JSON edits.
